zh.lifeSummary: 當Ellen de Chaworth於1190年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾沃特納爾 霍斯時，她的父親William Chaworth23歲，母親Alice de Alfreton20歲。她和Sir John Longvillers至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1263年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾宏比 卡素享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chaworth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Haworth (Yorkshire), probably from Old English haga ‘hedge, enclosure’ + worth ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Howarth
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Howarth in Rochdale (Lancashire), possibly from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + worth ‘enclosure’. The name may have been confused with Haworth .

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Ellen de Chaworth was born in 1190, in Watnall Chaworth, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, William Chaworth, was 23 and her mother, Alice de Alfreton, was 20. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir John Longvillers. She died in 1263, in Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir John Longvillers. Elle est décédée en 1263, à Hornby Castle, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Ellen de Chaworth(이)가 1190년 영국 노틴그함실르 왓널 차워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Chaworth(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Alfreton(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Longvillers(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1263년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 호른비 카스틀에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.
